stories.title: EU sanctions Russian cyber spies for years-long hacking
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Union on Monday blacklisted members of a Russian intelligence group it said were responsible for spying on and hacking targets across the EU and Ukraine from as early as 2010. A unit within Russia’s Federal Security Service (FSB) was responsible for directing the activities of the state-backed hacking group Turla, which has targeted governments, critical infrastructure and strategic industries across Europe, the EU’s top diplomat said in a statement . The FSB

stories.title: Forget being French president; Marine Le Pen is lucky not to be in prison. Why is she in public life at all? | Rokhaya Diallo
stories.summary: The far right leader’s career is not the issue: it’s whether a politician with her record has a legitimate right to seek office For many years, I have observed Marine Le Pen and her party and how they operate in France. I have heard their xenophobic, anti-immigrant rhetoric and felt it contaminate French political life. It is a rhetoric rooted in the history of a party founded by figures from France’s postwar far right. Nothing they do or say surprises me any more. But even by their standards th

stories.title: Sam Neill tributes: actors, directors and leaders remember ‘a true gentleman’
stories.summary: The star of more than 100 films is remembered as a champion of New Zealand’s arts, culture and environment, and a generous collaborator and friend • Sam Neill’s final interview • His 20 best performances • A life in pictures • ‘A true gentleman’: actors, directors and leaders pay tribute to Neill • Neill interviewed in 2023 , 2024 and 2026 Sam Neill’s friends, peers and admirers have rushed to pay tribute to the actor, after his sudden death on Monday at the age of 78. Neill’s co-star in A Long

stories.title: Brüsszeli útjai során többször egy antwerpeni luxusszállodában is béreltek szobát Nagy Mártonnak
stories.summary: Nem tudni arról, hogy dolga lett volna a kb. 45 kilométerre fekvő Antwerpenben.

stories.title: Gradual restart of news services on Hungarian public media begins
stories.summary: According to the announcement, the goal is "to provide the public with credible, fact-based, objective, and impartial information".

stories.title: Magyar Péter szerint a Fidesz és Sulyok alkotmánybírósági beadvánnyal fúrná meg a legújabb alkotmánymódosítást, reagált rá Gulyás is
stories.summary: Gulyás Gergely azt írta, hogy „az alkotmánymódosítás kapcsán rám, illetve a Fidesz-frakcióra vonatkozó valamennyi állítása valótlan”.

stories.title: A férjem szerint ez pornográfia – megnyílt Nagy-Britannia első romantasy könyvesboltja
stories.summary: Ellenségekből lett szerelmesek, csalafinta tündérek és erkölcsileg szürke sárkánylovasok. A fantasyt és a romantikát ötvöző alműfaj niche online trendből a könyvkiadás egyik legnagyobb kereskedelmi sikertörténete lett.

stories.title: Pörgős szójáték lett az év társasjátéka
stories.summary: Díjat ért a mukigyűjtögetés és Skócia újjáépítése is.

stories.title: 3,5 kilométeres magánút építésével vinnék a BYD-gyárból az autókat a dorozsmai vasútállomásra
stories.summary: A szegedi gyárban várhatóan ezer autó készülne naponta, ezek közül hétszázat a Waberer's vasúti terminálján tennének tehervonatokra. A Tiborcz István érdekeltségében lévő cég most is ellát a BYD-hoz kapcsolódó logisztikai feladatokat: több ezer konténer Kínából Magyarországra szállításában vesz részt.

stories.title: Szándékos gyújtogatás okozhatta a Párizs mellett tomboló erdőtüzet
stories.summary: A francia fővároshoz közeli erdőben vasárnap délután keletkezett tűz, a lángok azóta több mint 800 hektárnyi területet perzseltek fel.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor már úton van a vb-re, Frankfurtban találkoztak vele
stories.summary: Az elődöntőkön és a döntőn is részt vesz a korábbi miniszterelnök.

stories.title: „Uraim, önök mindent rosszul csinálnak” – átadták Európa legnagyobb Mercedes-gyárát Kecskeméten
stories.summary: Magyar Péter egy régi anekdotával avatta fel a Mercedes kecskeméti üzemeinek új csarnokait, a legenda szerint Barényi Béla magyar mérnök hosszan szapulta a Mercedest az állásinterjúján. Az új csarnokban a jövőben az elektromos C-osztályt gyártják majd.

stories.title: Az nem férhet bele, hogy ezek után Orbán megjelenjen a vb-meccseken – élő parlamenti közvetítés és elemzés
stories.summary: Hétfőn elfogadja a parlament az Alaptörvény 17. módosítását, ezzel Sulyok sorsa végleg megpecsételődhet. A Fidesz kivonult a parlamentből, Toroczkai László pedig helyzetbe kerülhet. Hétfőn is közvetítjük a parlamentet, vendégünk Róna Dániel, a 21 Kutatóközpont vezetője, majd Sarkadi Zsolt, a Telex újságírója.

stories.title: Papának annyi mondanivalója van, mégsem ismeri el a zeneipar
stories.summary: Harmadik budapesti koncertjét adta Morrissey, a kultikus Smiths zenekar egykori alapító-frontembere, aki szentül hiszi, hogy a zeneipar végleg cserben hagyta.

stories.title: Nagy nap ez a mai! Ön mától szabad ember! – mondta Gulyás Gergelynek Magyar, aki a beszéde közben értesült a Fidesz-frakcióvezető lemondásáról
stories.summary: Magyar Péter Sulyok Tamás hibáit sorolta fel a parlamentben az elmozdítását célzó Alaptörvény-módosítás elfogadása előtt. A Fidesz szerint a mai a demokrácia gyásznapja, bojkottálják az ülést, Gulyás Gergely pedig lemondott a frakcióvezetésről. Parlament percről percre.

stories.title: “Gentlemen, you’re doing everything wrong”—Europe’s biggest Mercedes plant opens in Kecskemét
stories.summary: Péter Magyar cited an old anecdote about Hungarian engineer Béla Barényi at the opening ceremony for the new production halls at Mercedes’ Kecskemét plant. The new facilities will be used to manufacture electric C-Class models.

stories.title: Gulyás Gergely lemond a frakcióvezetői tisztségről
stories.summary: Sem a Fidesz-, sem a KDNP-frakció nem vesz részt az Alaptörvény 17. módosításáról szóló szavazáson hétfőn, és Gulyás Gergely a lemondását is bejelentette a Fidesz frakcióvezetői posztjáról hétfőn, a Parlamentben tartott sajtótájékoztatón. Rétvári Bence gyásznapnak, a magyar demokrácia fekete napjának nevezte a szavazás napját.

stories.title: Sulyok Tamás szerint Magyar Péter valótlan állításokkal manipulálja a közvéleményt
stories.summary: A köztársasági elnök szerint a hamis tényközlés sérti az alkotmányos rendet.

stories.title: Videón, ahogy el-elcsukló hangon mond köszönetet Kátai-Németh Vilmosnak Magyar Péter
stories.summary: „Mit üzen nekik a Fidesz–KDNP azzal, hogy azért tesz egy vak embert gúny tárgyává, mert nem az ő oldalukon áll?” – kérdezte beszédében a miniszterelnök.

stories.title: Magyar Péter kommentben reagált Sulyok Tamás vádjára
stories.summary: „Vegyen példát Gulyás Gergelyről elnök úr!” – írta a miniszterelnök a köztársasági elnök bejegyzése alá.

stories.title: Slaven Bilić lett az új horvát szövetségi kapitány
stories.summary: Bilić 2006–12 között már irányította Horvátországot.

stories.title: Karácsony Gergely: Nem most lenne ideje elengedni az augusztus 20-ai tűzijátékot, lehetőleg mindörökre?
stories.summary: Budapest főpolgármestere szerint a tűzijátékra költött közpénznek is lenne jobb helye, és a környezetnek is jobb lenne, ha elmaradna a tűzijátékozás.

stories.title: Fidesz parliamentary group leader resigns his post
stories.summary: Neither the Fidesz nor the KDNP parliamentary groups will participate in Monday’s vote on the 17th amendment to the Fundamental Law of Hungary. Additionally, Gergely Gulyás has announced his resignation as Fidesz parliamentary group leader on Monday during a press conference held in Parliament. KDNP's group leader called the day of the vote a day of mourning and a black day for Hungarian democracy.

stories.title: Róna Dániel: Nem látom a varázstrükköt, ami kirángatná a Fideszt ebből a gödörből
stories.summary: A 21 Kutatóközpont elemzője gyengének látja a Fideszt eddig ellenzéki szerepben, Orbán Viktort pedig csapdahelyzetben. Az elemző szerint a Mi Hazánk gesztusai halálos ölelést jelenthetnek a Fidesznek.

stories.title: Was Erdoğan's gun gift a faux pas or an old-school diplomatic tradition?
stories.summary: When NATO leaders opened their gift boxes from the Turkish president and found a .357 Magnum revolver, some panicked and others laughed. But gifting heads of state lavish, personalised firearms is a tradition as old as Samuel Colt — and as recent as a Czech pistol presented to Trump in 2019.

stories.title: Poland could cut arms aid to Ukraine as WWII spat continues
stories.summary: As Poland observes a day of remembrance for the victims of a Ukrainian nationalist military unit, there are increasing signs that it could cut off its neighbor from military aid, even as the war against Russia continues.

stories.title: Newly released footage shows revolver gifted by Erdogan to NATO leaders
stories.summary: Images shared by the Lithuanian Presidency show a revolver given by Turkey's Erdogan to every NATO leader as a parting gift after the summit in Ankara. Each leader received a box containing a revolver engraved with their name, six bullets and a note exempting the weapons from export control.

stories.title: Hungary public media reform begins with black screen apology
stories.summary: People tuning in to public service media in Hungary on Tuesday were greeted by a black screen and an apology. Peter Magyar has begun dismantling one of Viktor Orban's most potent tools of power.

stories.title: France fans erupt as Les Bleus down Morocco to reach semis
stories.summary: Scenes of jubilation unfolded in Paris and at Gillette Stadium in Foxborough, Massachusetts, as Les Bleus beat Morocco 2-0 on 09 July to secure a place in the FIFA World Cup semi-finals.

stories.title: Swedish MEP files police complaint accusing Danish colleague of racist hate speech
stories.summary: Abir Al-Sahlani targeted on social media after condemning anti-immigration chants in European parliament A Swedish MEP has filed a police complaint accusing a fellow MEP of racist hate speech after she was targeted on social media over her condemnation of far-right, anti-immigration chants in the European parliament. The complaint, which was filed last week with police in Sweden, relates to the aftermath last month of the decision by some rightwing MEPs to erupt in chants of “send them back” fol

stories.title: Traffic through strait of Hormuz plummets after US and Iran trade strikes – Middle East crisis live
stories.summary: Trump claims US will become ‘the guardian of the strait’ after reportedly the lowest levels of crossings in a month US launches new attack wave on Iran as Tehran says diplomacy has proven ‘futile’ Bahrain’s military has accused Iran of targeting civilians with its latest attacks on the country, after Tehran said it had struck US military facilities and infrastructure there earlier. “Iran continues its systematic hostile approach through its heinous attacks with missiles and drones that target ci

stories.title: US launches more strikes on Iran as renewed standoff over Strait of Hormuz intensifies
stories.summary: Iranian state media reported US strikes targeting large areas across southern and western Iran, including Qeshm island and Bandar Abbas near the strait, and in Khuzestan province bordering Iraq. Missile alert sirens also sounded at dawn Monday in Bahrain, home to the US Navy's 5th Fleet.

stories.title: Macron trumpets high defense spending in last speech to the troops
stories.summary: PARIS — French President Emmanuel Macron warned against nationalism and made a last plea for deeper European defense cooperation Monday in his final speech to the armed forces before leaving office next year. “Wherever nationalism is pandered to, whether in France or elsewhere, we are misunderstanding our own history,” Macron said. “At a time when Europe is rearming, to think that building up capabilities separately is the way forward is absurd.” This was Macron’s 1

stories.title: UK will proscribe Iran’s IRGC over ‘threats to life’
stories.summary: LONDON — The U.K. government announced Monday it will proscribe Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps. In a statement, Security Minister Angela Eagle said the U.K. has “identified activity linked to the IRGC involving threats to life and intimidation on U.K. soil.” The IRGC’s “role extends far beyond that of a conventional military force,” Eagle said. “It encompasses intelligence activity, the use of proxy actors, and the projection of influence designed to advance Ira

stories.title: Queensland unlawfully detained children in adult watch house without private toilet access, tribunal finds
stories.summary: State ordered to apologise after ruling finds it breached law when detaining teenagers in a Cairns watch house Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Queensland government broke the law and violated the human rights of three children by detaining them in an adult-only watch house without private toilet access or clean clothes, according to a tribunal judgment. The case was brought by three teenagers separately detained in the Cairns watch house between June 2021 and 202

stories.summary: Tehran says latest US attacks have ‘rendered futile’ the diplomatic efforts of the last few months. Plus, hit song in Australia prompts speculation about AI use Don’t already get First Thing in your inbox? Sign up here Good morning. stories.title: UK to list Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps as terrorist organisation
stories.summary: Home Office announces ban on support for Iranian military group in escalation of diplomatic tensions with Tehran The UK will list Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps as a terrorist organisation, ministers have announced, in a major escalation of the diplomatic tensions with Tehran. The Home Office said on Monday it would ban support for the IRGC, a central branch of the Iranian military, after years of political division over the issue . The move is equivalent to proscription, though not le

stories.title: Burnham expected to vote in favour of Mahmood’s asylum system changes
stories.summary: Home secretary also set to outline plans to enable deportation of grooming gang ringleader Shabir Ahmed UK politics live – latest updates Andy Burnham is expected to vote for the immigration bill on Monday night in the Commons, with his team indicating that he backs Shabana Mahmood’s changes for indefinite leave to remain (ILR) and on refugee settlement. The bill returns to the Commons on Monday for its second reading, where Mahmood is also expected to announce plans to make it possible to depor

stories.title: US and Iran exchange fresh wave of strikes as Tehran says diplomacy has proven ‘futile’
stories.summary: Tit-for-tat missile and drone attacks continue over control of strait of Hormuz, increasing pressure on truce • Middle East crisis live – latest updates US and Iranian military forces exchanged missile and drone strikes over the weekend and into Monday, as Tehran ⁠targeted US bases in the region and announced it was denying passage of ships through the ​strait of Hormuz. Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said it had targeted US military facilities in Bahrain and Kuwait, destroyed r

stories.title: Ukraine updates: 'Coalition of the willing' meeting in Paris
stories.summary: Ukraine's allies are meeting in Paris for talks on continued military support for Kyiv. Meanwhile the EU's top diplomat said there's no consensus yet on more Russian sanctions. DW has more.

stories.title: EU wants 13+ age restriction for social media
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Union will impose a minimum age for young internet users to access social media without parental supervision, Commission President Ursula von der Leyen announced on Monday. “It is very clear that we need age-appropriate restrictions to platforms. This is not about whether children can access social media. It is about whether and when social media can access our children,” she said. Von der Leyen presented a report by a panel of experts she convened last year t

stories.title: Belgium slams EU Commission for inaction against illegal Israeli settlements
stories.summary: Belgium’s foreign minister accused the European Commission on Monday of taking “insufficient” action against trade with illegal Israeli settlements as a push to clamp down divides EU countries. EU foreign ministers gathered in Brussels are due to discuss a paper written by Ursula von der Leyen’s EU executive, and seen by POLITICO , that pitches various options for limiting trade with the West Bank settlements, including “prohibitive tariffs” and a licensing sy

stories.title: Bruxelles veut limiter à 13 ans l’accès aux réseaux sociaux
stories.summary: BRUXELLES — L’Union européenne va fixer un âge minimum à partir duquel les jeunes internautes pourront accéder aux réseaux sociaux sans la surveillance de leurs parents, a annoncé ce lundi 13 juillet la présidente de la Commission. Ursula von der Leyen présentait ce matin les conclusions du groupe d’experts qu’elle avait chargé, l’an dernier, de faire des propositions pour lutter contre les effets néfastes des réseaux sociaux. Leur rapport, publié ce jour, recommande que

stories.title: Iran launches retaliatory attacks after fresh US strikes – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog has now closed – you can read our latest report from the Middle East here Bahrain’s interior ministry instructed residents to take shelter after attacks on the island nation as Iran targets US interests in the Gulf. The siren has been sounded... citizens and residents are urged to remain calm and head to the nearest safe place,” the ministry posted on X. Continue reading...

stories.summary: Royal commission on antisemitism and social cohesion hears lived experiences of Jewish students and academics Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast An academic at UNSW was subjected to Nazi salutes by students in his business class, the royal commission into antisemitism and social cohesion has heard. The fourth block of hearings, taking place in Melbourne this week, is examining the lived experiences of Jewish student

stories.title: Trump’s Nato remarks rang hollow – but he recognizes something real | Christopher S Chivvis
stories.summary: US political leaders must be more clear-eyed about our global alliances, without embracing his scorched-earth approach Donald Trump memorably took out a full-page advertisement in multiple newspapers in 1987 charging that America was carrying too much weight for its allies. In his first term he repeated this charge, threatening to withdraw from Nato and berating US allies around the world in the process. Last week’s gathering of Nato’s heads of government in Turkey suggests his approach is runni

stories.title: Keystone pipeline operator agrees to pay $26.9m penalty over Kansas oil spill
stories.summary: Proposed legal settlement over 2022 oil spill would resolve allegations that South Bow violated clean water laws A proposed legal settlement with the US government would require the Keystone pipeline system’s operator to pay a $26.9m civil penalty over a large oil spill in Kansas in December 2022 and spend about $40m more to prevent future accidents. The agreement would resolve allegations from the US Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) and Kansas that South Bow, based in Canada, violated US a

stories.title: Firefighting planes scrambled from south of France to tackle huge wildfire near Paris
stories.summary: Officials say blaze in Fontainebleau forest is of ‘exceptional scale’, with 900 homes evacuated and road and rail links hit Europe live – latest updates French firefighters are tackling a blaze of unprecedented scale sweeping through Fontainebleau forest south-east of Paris, as the Spanish prime minister visited the scene of a deadly wildfire in southern Spain and warned: “The climate emergency kills.” The fire began late on Sunday afternoon in the one-time royal hunting preserve about 40 miles

stories.title: EU chief pledges social media ban to protect children from ‘predatory algorithms’
stories.summary: Ursula von der Leyen’s commitment comes after panel of experts calls for restriction for under-13s The European Commission president, Ursula von der Leyen, has pledged an EU-wide social media ban for children after an expert group called for restrictions for those under 13. “It is clear we need age-appropriate restrictions to platforms,” von der Leyen told reporters after the publication of a report on child safety online. Continue reading...

stories.title: Ukraine to present its anti-ballistic programme at Paris meeting today, Zelenskyy says – Europe live
stories.summary: Emmanuel Macron hosts ‘coalition of the willing’ in Paris amid hopes that Ukraine’s recent advances could force Putin towards negotiations in Kyiv Meanwhile, Russia has been forced to suspend shipping in the Sea of Azov after 90 vessels were targeted by Ukrainian drones in less than a week. Continue reading...

stories.title: Person reported killed during ICE operation in Maine, state house speaker says – US politics live
stories.summary: Ryan Fecteau says person was reportedly shot in Biddeford, with authorities on the scene to investigate Sign up for the Breaking News US email The Democrat’s outgoing senator for Michigan Gary Peters has endorsed member of Congress Haley Stevens to be his successor over Abdul El-Sayed in the state’s neck-and-neck primary race set for 4 August. “She has demonstrated to me time and time again that she’s a fighter,” Peters told the Detroit News . “We need workhorses in the Senate, and we need someo

stories.title: Renewed US-Iran war is hitting Gulf countries hard
stories.summary: After US strikes on Iran, Iran has targeted US allies in the Middle East. Even if Iran isn't targeting the governments or populations of its neighbors, Gulf countries have much to lose as fighting escalates.

stories.title: World Cup: Balogun red and expansion keep FIFA in spotlight
stories.summary: The fallout from Folarin Balogun's red card reversal continues as reports suggest the chairman of FIFA’s disciplinary committee made the decision unilaterally. Meanwhile, the World Cup could be set to expand once more.

stories.title: South Africa says more than 53,000 foreigners deported in migration campaign
stories.summary: South Africa is cracking down on undocumented migrants following widespread anti-immigration protests.

stories.title: Counterterrorism police probe Ann Widdecombe death
stories.summary: LONDON — Counterterrorism police will lead the investigation into the death of former Conservative minister Ann Widdecombe. Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood said in a post on X Monday that police are now considering a terrorist motive for the attack.  Police had previously said that there was no information to suggest there was a political motivation, and that they had ruled out treating Widdecombe’s death as a terrorist incident.  Widdecombe, 78, was found dead in her home in Devo

stories.title: Jared Kushner joins EU Gaza donor meeting as Brussels unveils €900M package
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Jared Kushner joined a major EU-backed conference on Gaza by video link Monday as Brussels launched a nearly €900 million international initiative aimed at restoring essential services and beginning the territory’s early recovery. The meeting of the Palestine Donor Group brings together 65 delegations and launched the Team Gaza Initiative, with an initial package of €883.6 million. The funding will support water and sanitation, waste removal, health care, energy, agriculture and food

stories.title: Mastermind of €88m Louvre heist thought they ‘could have taken more’
stories.summary: Alleged thieves in October 2025 robbery damaged a gem-encrusted crown worn in the 19th century by Empress Eugénie Two men suspected of making off with €88m (£75m) worth of crown jewels from the Louvre museum in Paris last October have reportedly told investigators that the alleged mastermind behind the heist was disappointed by the haul and thought “they could have taken more”. The French newspaper Le Monde cited transcripts of the alleged thieves’ questioning last month by two investigating jud

stories.title: More than 120 families contacted as police investigate alleged abuse by Sydney childcare worker
stories.summary: Police allege there are 136 victims of the man’s offending, which allegedly took place over a 16-year period Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australian federal police have contacted more than 120 families as they investigate alleged offending by a former Sydney childcare worker. The man, 35, has been charged with 329 offences allegedly committed over a 16-year period against 136 victims. Continue reading...

stories.title: Zaghari-Ratcliffe condemns Iran’s ‘cruel’ rearrest of wildlife activist couple
stories.summary: Nazanin Zaghari-Ratcliffe was in prison with Sepideh Kashani, who worked with husband Houman Jokar to save Asiatic cheetah Nazanin Zaghari-Ratcliffe has described the rearrest of two Iranian environmentalists, one of whom she met at Evin prison, as “unimaginably cruel and alarming”. Husband and wife Houman Jokar and Sepideh Kashani were arrested by the ministry of intelligence at their home on 1 July. No reason has been given and their whereabouts are unknown. Continue reading...

stories.title: Weather tracker: Unusually warm rivers affect French nuclear power plants
stories.summary: High temperatures and below average rainfall put pressure on waterways used to cool reactors Above average temperatures combined with below average rainfall across much of western and central Europe during June and the first half of July have placed increasing pressure on rivers, ecosystems and energy infrastructure. Persistent high pressure brought prolonged sunshine, suppressed rainfall and enhanced evaporation, causing river levels to fall and water temperatures to increase. These unusually w

stories.title: Chatsworth House pilots ‘community membership’ free entry scheme
stories.summary: Initiative with Derbyshire Libraries aims to boost access to cultural experiences and ‘champion reading for pleasure’ When Kate, a 47-year-old contract worker came face to face with Charlotte Brontë’s handwriting while visiting Chatsworth House, the avid reader, who counts Jane Eyre as her favourite book, struggled to contain her excitement. “I had a little bit of a moment,” she said. “I just thought: ‘Wow, that was actually Charlotte Brontë’s writing there on that page.’ That was pretty special

stories.title: Plan to restore nature in England by 2030 criticised as ‘completely insufficient’
stories.summary: Critics accuse ministers of failing to take control of nature crisis and leaving it to private landowners to act voluntarily The government’s plan to protect and restore nature in England by 2030 has been condemned as “pathetic” and “completely insufficient” in the face of the spiralling environmental crisis. The long-awaited plan published on Monday calls for landowners to voluntarily opt to protect and enhance nature, rather than creating legal protections for nature across more of the country

stories.title: Ann Widdecombe death: counter-terrorism police take over investigation
stories.summary: Officers say decision made after ‘new information and evidence has come to light’ Counter-terrorism police are now leading the investigation into the death of the former MP and Reform spokesperson Ann Widdecombe in light of “new information and evidence”. Widdecombe’s body was found with serious injuries by the ambulance service at her home in Haytor, Devon, at 11.40am on Thursday, Devon and Cornwall police said. Continue reading...

stories.title: Young men report more ‘sextortion’ than any other age group, Australia’s online safety watchdog says
stories.summary: In six months last year, more than 2,000 such complaints were made to eSafety Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A new report by Australia’s online safety regulator has found “significant gaps” in how major tech platforms tackle online sexual extortion and child sexual exploitation, as “reports of this abuse continue to rise”. The findings come from eSafety’s latest transparency report, examining how tech companies – including Apple, Meta, Google, Microsoft, Snap, Disco

stories.title: Trump officials accused of stacking top chemical safety board with industry ‘mouthpieces’
stories.summary: Public health advocates warn of conflicts of interests and say panel likely to provide justification for key rollbacks The Trump administration has stacked a top chemical safety board with industry-aligned scientists who have a range of financial conflicts of interest and stand to profit from deregulation, public health advocates say. The Environmental Protection Agency’s science advisory committee on chemicals (SACC) is slated to review research for dozens of toxic chemicals during the new memb

stories.title: At least 27 killed in fire at Bangkok pub with another 22 critically injured
stories.summary: The fire is one of the deadliest such incidents in the popular tourist destination in ⁠recent years An explosive fire at a popular pub in Thailand’s ⁠capital, Bangkok, has killed 27 ⁠people and left another 22 ​in critical condition, in one of the deadliest such incidents in the tourism hub in recent years. Officials said they were investigating whether emergency exits may have been obstructed, hindering people from escaping the burning Rong Beer Na Lat Phrao pub. Continue reading...

stories.title: VW chief confirms plan to cut 50,000 jobs as board rejects plant closures
stories.summary: Oliver Blume tells staff restructuring proposal includes ‘controversial decisions’ but he has broad support Business live – latest updates The chief executive of Volkswagen has confirmed plans to cut 50,000 more jobs despite the carmaker’s supervisory board rejecting his plan to shut four factories in Germany. Oliver Blume told staff on Monday that proposals for a sprawling restructuring was “the most comprehensive realignment in the company’s history” and revolved around “12 initiatives, approx

stories.title: Pilot fighting Colorado wildfire dies as plane crashes into reservoir
stories.summary: Body of pilot involved in tackling Gold Mountain fire recovered by divers from Silver Jack reservoir A pilot who was helping to fight a wildfire in Colorado has died after the aviator’s aircraft crashed into a reservoir, local authorities said. The Gunnison county sheriff’s office said in a statement that it was notified of the deadly crash at about 5.17pm local time on Sunday. Gunnison’s regional communications center received a call reporting that the aircraft involved in the crash went down i

stories.title: Killings continue on Del Monte farm in Kenya, families say, after G4S hired for security
stories.summary: Exclusive: Three men killed in incidents over past year allegedly involving G4S guards, who replaced in-house team after previous deaths Bereaved families and politicians have raised alarm about continued killings on Del Monte’s pineapple farm in Kenya despite the company hiring G4S to replace its in-house security team after previous deaths were exposed by the Guardian. Del Monte appointed G4S to guard the farm, which is estimated to cover at least 40 sq km, the area of a small city, after the

stories.title: Lindsey Graham tributes from Israel and Ukraine point to complicated, often bloody legacy
stories.summary: Republican senator, who died Saturday, had a global reach few could rival and was vital in shaping Trump’s worldview It was revealing that one of the first tributes to Lindsey Graham, a US senator who died on Saturday aged 71, came from Itamar Ben-Gvir, Israel’s national security minister, a far-right provocateur who recently caused widespread anger by sharing footage of himself taunting bound activists who had been trying to sail to Gaza with aid. Benjamin Netanyahu, the Israeli prime minister,

stories.title: Sam Neill, Jurassic Park actor, dies 'unexpectedly' at 78
stories.summary: New Zealand actor Sam Neill achieved international fame playing Dr. Alan Grant in the 1993 film Jurassic Park. But his career included many other notable TV and film appearances.

stories.title: EU approves new passenger rights for air travel
stories.summary: EU ministers have approved a set of reforms giving more rights to air travelers. Among other things, compensation claims are to be made easier.

stories.title: India news: Repatriation underway after Vietnam boat sinking
stories.summary: The remains of the Indian tourists killed in the speedboat accident in Vietnam are set to arrive in Mumbai. Meanwhile, the top court heard a plea on a court-monitored probe into alleged the Ram Mandir donation fraud.

stories.title: Germany: Iraqi sentenced to life for enslaving Yazidi girls
stories.summary: The man and his former partner are accused of holding two Yazidi girls captive for years while subjecting them to forced labor, abuse and torture before handing them over to other IS members.

stories.title: Germany news: Tobacco tax to rise more than planned by 2030
stories.summary: The ruling coalition said the hike is not only to fill a budget gap but also to protect public health. Meanwhile, another US national suffering from Ebola has landed in Germany and is receiving treatment. Follow DW.

stories.title: EU experts urge social media restrictions for kids under 13
stories.summary: An expert panel has recommended limiting access to social media for young children across the EU. The European Commission is expected to present a proposal for legislation on the issue in the coming months.

stories.title: Kenya: What impact will Dangote's new refinery have on the region?
stories.summary: Is Kenya Africa's next major energy hub? A plan from Dangote Industries to build a massive refinery off the country's coast could reshape fuel supply, trade and economic growth across East Africa.

stories.title: Iran: Living in limbo between war and ceasefire
stories.summary: Repeated ceasefire violations, contradictory political signals and ongoing emergency measures have fueled exhaustion, anger and deep uncertainty among many Iranians.

stories.title: France opens terror probe after gun found in Jewish suburb
stories.summary: Police said they found a "military-grade weapon" during the search of a suspicious car parked in a Paris neighborhood with a large Jewish population.

stories.title: Iran war: US carries out fresh strikes near Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The US army said the strikes near the vital waterway were intended to "degrade" Tehran's ability to attack commercial ships. Iran has retaliated by targeting US allies in the region. Follow DW for the latest.

stories.title: Watch: At the scene where Bangkok's deadly bar fire unfolded
stories.summary: The BBC’s South East Asia correspondent visits the site where at least 27 people were killed during a bar fire in Thailand.

stories.title: Fire at pub on outskirts of Bangkok kills at least 27 people and injures dozens
stories.summary: The venue has a stage, two bars and a kitchen, with a total capacity of more than 300 people, as well as four fire exits, according to a Facebook post promoting a live show this month.

stories.title: Heavy rains and flash floods in Bangladesh leave 51 dead
stories.summary: Thousands have lost their homes as flash floods and landslides have hit large parts of the country.

stories.title: Major wildfire rages south of Paris during heatwave
stories.summary: A major wildfire has spread through France's Fontainebleau forest south of Paris, prompting a large emergency response, disrupting road and rail traffic, and forcing authorities to deploy firefighting aircraft.

stories.title: Venezuela quake death toll rises to 4,490, officials say, but no number given for missing
stories.summary: Camps for families left homeless have sprung up in stadiums, plazas and on sidewalks in La Guaira as well as nearby Caracas.

stories.title: Azerbaijan considering full withdrawal from Council of Europe, Aliyev says
stories.summary: President Ilham Aliyev said Baku was considering full withdrawal from the 46-member organisation after Azerbaijan's voting rights in PACE were suspended in 2024.

stories.title: Hollywood blockbusters and arthouse films: Sam Neill's best-known roles in pictures
stories.summary: The beloved actor's career ranged from box office blockbusters to thrillers and award-winning dramas.

stories.title: Senator Lindsey Graham died of aortic tear, examiner says
stories.summary: Graham, a close Republican ally of Donald Trump, died at the age of 71 on Saturday evening.

stories.title: People flee Bangkok bar as huge flames shoot out from doorway
stories.summary: At least 28 people have died and 25 are critically injured after a fire tore through a bar in a popular Bangkok district.

stories.title: Wildfire raging south of Paris could have been set deliberately, says minister
stories.summary: It was the first time firefighting planes had been sent up from the normally drier and hotter south of the country to tackle fires in the Paris region.

stories.title: Men disguised as police kill Ecuadorean drug lord's brother
stories.summary: The victim, David Macías, was the brother of drug lord 'El Fito', who is awaiting trial in the US.

stories.title: Drone footage shows devastation after deadly Spain wildfire
stories.summary: Drone footage shows the devastation left by a deadly wildfire in southern Spain that killed at least 12 people and burned around 7,000 hectares. Authorities say the blaze has now been brought under control.

stories.title: German court convicts Iraqi couple of enslaving Yazidi girls
stories.summary: The pair, named only as Twana H.S. and Asia R. A., left Germany for Iraq in 2015 and became members of the Islamic State group.

stories.title: The SpaceX IPO made history. One month on has it lost momentum?
stories.summary: A month on from the firm's stock market debut, the reality of how SpaceX currently makes money has seemed to come into clearer focus.

stories.title: 'Hero, legend, sweetheart': Tributes to Jurassic Park actor Sam Neill, who has died aged 78
stories.summary: Friends, politicians and fellow film stars remember Neill following his "sudden and unexpected" death.

stories.title: At least 28 killed, 25 critically injured after fire engulfs Bangkok bar
stories.summary: Firefighters arrived at the scene in the Chatuchak district to find patrons running through flames to escape.

stories.title: Video shows civilians taking cover during Russian strike on Sumy
stories.summary: Four people were injured after a Russian strike hit Odesa on 13 July 2026, according to Ukraine's State Emergency Service.

stories.title: Kremlin accuses EU of canceling Russian culture after budget cuts to Venice Biennale
stories.summary: The Kremlin accused the European Commission of attempting to “cancel” Russian culture after the EU announced plans to cut funding for one of the continent’s most prestigious cultural platforms. Commission Executive Vice-President Henna Virkkunen announced Saturday that she had asked the European Education and Culture Executive Agency (EACEA) to terminate the Venice Biennale’s €2 million EU grant, as culture in Europe should promote democratic values — something “not respected in toda

stories.title: EU defense remains heavily reliant on the United States
stories.summary: There are many obstacles as EU governments seek to reduce their dependence on the US. DW looks at the European Unions's attempts to develop counterparts to Patriot and Tomahawk missiles, F-35 jets, and Starlink internet.

stories.title: Are German companies leaving the country?
stories.summary: Should I stay or should I go? Given Germany's high cost and sluggish growth, some business leaders are mulling relocating, or at least investing abroad. DW has a look at the numbers.

stories.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy swaps out prime minister in reshuffle
stories.summary: Ukraine's president announced a government shake up, with PM Yuliia Svyrydenko stepping down after a year in office.

stories.title: Sinner beats Zverev to retain Wimbledon title
stories.summary: Jannik Sinner retained his Wimbledon title by beating Alexander Zverev in Sunday's men's final. The Italian claims his fifth Grand Slam crown and extends his dominance over the German to 10 straight victories.

stories.title: Venezuela: Almost 4,500 killed in quake, many more missing
stories.summary: As the official death toll from the devastating earthquakes which struck northern Venezuela last month continues to rise, authorities are planning temporary housing to shelter thousands of people left homeless.

stories.title: Thailand: Bangkok bar fire leaves dozens dead
stories.summary: At least 27 people were killed in a fire at a bar in the Thai capital, Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul told local media. More than 60 others were injured in the fire.

stories.title: 'This landscape is completely charred': Inside the village at epicentre of Spain's wildfires
stories.summary: The BBC's Nick Beake sees homes razed to the ground and "a sea of black" in Bédar.

stories.title: Former Emir of Qatar Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani dies aged 74
stories.summary: He took power in 1996 after staging a bloodless coup against his father, and went on to transform Qatar into the gas-rich power it is today.

stories.title: France: hundreds dive into the Seine for Paris’ first river race in 80 years
stories.summary: Swimmers plunged into the Seine near the Eiffel Tower for the Open Swim Harmonie Mutuelle, the first major organised race on the river in nearly 80 years.

stories.title: Israel to hold elections on 27 October in what is seen as referendum on Netanyahu leadership
stories.summary: Israel will hold national elections on 27 October, the last date allowed by law to hold national elections. The Knesset is set to end its current term on 17 July.

stories.title: Spain wildfire: Firefighters gain ground as Almería evacuees return home
stories.summary: Firefighters gained the upper hand on the deadly Almería wildfire after days of battling the flames, as the blaze that killed 12 people and burned 66 square kilometres began to ease, allowing some of the nearly 1,500 evacuees to return home.

stories.title: New era for Gibraltar with removal of 118-year-old border controls with Spain
stories.summary: It is hoped that free movement between the UK territory and Spain will provide an economic boost.

stories.title: US Senator Mitch McConnell says absence due to fall and pneumonia
stories.summary: The veteran Republican says he will not return to work "quite yet", in his first statement following weeks of speculation about his health.

stories.title: The Chinese graduate accused of being Mexico's 'fentanyl king'
stories.summary: Cartel members and ex-colleagues describe how Zhang Zhidong allegedly linked Chinese chemicals to Mexican drug labs.

stories.title: 10 years of Pokémon Go and the millions still trying to catch 'em all
stories.summary: Michael Steranka, vice president at the mobile game's owner Scopely, says it has always been about bringing people together.

stories.title: How Indian scientists are mapping the brain's last frontier
stories.summary: India's new brainstem atlas offers scientists an unprecedented map of one of the brain's least known regions.

stories.title: British couple return to village at heart of deadly Spanish wildfire
stories.summary: At least 13 people, including five believed to be Britons, were killed by Thursday's wildfire in Spain's Almeria province.

stories.title: Hegedűs Zsolt: Sulyok Tamás a hallgatásával bukott meg
stories.summary: Az egészségügyi miniszter arról osztotta meg a gondolatait, hogy miért kell eltávolítani a köztársasági elnököt a hivatalából. Az Alaptörvény 17. módosításáról hétfő este szavaz az országgyűlés.

stories.title: Meghalt Sam Neill, a Jurassic Park színésze
stories.summary: Az új-zélandi színész 78 éves volt, korábban rákkal küzdött, de a család szerint nem ez okozta a halálát.

stories.title: Azt hitték, vízbe fulladt, de helyette átúszta a Soroksári-Dunát, és maga hívott mentőt egy ittas ember
stories.summary: Vasárnap este kerestek egy otthonról ittasan eltűnt személyt a Pest Vármegyei Kutató-Mentő Szolgálat munkatársai.

stories.title: A francia csapatban nincs egy francia sem a volt spanyol kormányfő szerint, felháborodtak a politikusok
stories.summary: Mariano Rajoy okozott újabb rasszista botrányt egy paraguayi szenátor botrányos kijelentése után.

stories.summary: Egy hét alatt kell találnia rendezvényszervezőt, aki lebonyolítja a programsorozatot.

stories.summary: A DNS-technológia használata 1988-ban még nem volt rutinszerű, így úszhatta meg évtizedekig a gyilkosságot.

stories.summary: A salvadori Iván Bartont latin-amerikai és skandináv stáb támogatja majd.

stories.summary: Egy, a Tisza volt rendezvényigazgatójának érdekeltségébe tartozó céggel is kötöttek szerződést.

stories.summary: Idén csaknem nyolcszor akkora összegben hiteleztek a bankok a lakástámogatással vásárlóknak, mint egy évvel korábban.

stories.summary: Egyik legfontosabb exportútvonalát adta fel ezzel. Hétfőn ukrán dróntámadás érte a moszkvai régiót is, hárman meghaltak.

stories.title: Diego Forlán veszi át az uruguayi válogatott vezetését
stories.summary: A vb az uruguayi futball mohácsa volt a hamari kieséssel, a csatárlegendának 2027-ig kéne gatyába rázni a csapatot.

stories.summary: Zhang Zhidong állítólag fontos szerepet játszott a hálózatban, ő szerezte be a fentanil előállításához szükséges prekurzor vegyszereket, amelyeket Kínából szállítottak Mexikóba.

stories.title: Amit a billentyűzet nem tud: ezért írunk ma is kézzel

Emailek, üzenetek, közösségi média posztok, prezentációk, online dokumentumok és a mesterséges intelligencia által generált tartalmak tömege vesz körül bennünket – közben a kézírás lassan kiszorul a mindennapokból.

stories.summary: A gazdasági élénkítés lehetőségeivel foglalkozott az Európai Bizottság friss jelentése, ami már csak 0,9 százalékos gazdasági növekedést jósol az idei évre az eurózónában.

stories.summary: A nő a lakás pontos címét megjelölte, csakhogy a megadott házszám alatt még épület sincs Nagykanizsán.

stories.summary: A betörők tavaly októberben több mint 30 milliárd forint értékű ékszerrel lógtak meg a párizsi múzeumból.

stories.summary: Könnyen kifejlődik a ház körüli kisebb vizekben, locsoláskor edényalátétben megálló vízben. Összeszedtük a legérdekesebb dolgokat az NNGYK szúnyogkisokosából.

stories.summary: A hosszú, lenge, széles szárú nadrág sorra szedi „áldozatait”.

stories.summary: Ismét bedobta a FIFA elnöke a létszámemelést, pedig sokan már a mostani, 48 csapatos formát is túlzónak tartják. Több kontinentális fociszövetség tiltakozott már korábban, a FIFA viszont sok-sok dollár bevételt vizionál.

stories.summary: Erling Haaland-ék ugyan kiestek a vb-negyeddöntőben, szinte győztesnek kijáró fogadást kapnak majd a norvég fővárosban.

stories.summary: Az ügy előzménye, hogy vicsorgó vak embernek nevezte a minisztert Ábrahám Róbert fideszes influenszer, a Pesti Srácok volt munkatársa egy nyilvános rendezvényen.

stories.title: Az egyik legmakacsabb lakossági beidegződést is megtörte az erős forint
stories.summary: Továbbra is jóval magasabbnak érzékeljük az inflációt a valósnál, a várakozások azonban már a hivatalos inflációs célnál és előrejelzésnél is mélyebbre süllyedtek, amire rég nem volt példa.

stories.title: Három év után Jay-Z koncertjén tért vissza a színpadra Rihanna
stories.summary: Az énekesnő utoljára a 2023-as Super Bowlon lépett fel, azóta csak egy indiai luxusesküvőn hallhatták a szerencsés vendégek.

stories.summary: A Fidesz kommunikációs igazgatója szerint a lemondás a Fidesz gyengeségét sugallja, és meghátrálásnak tűnhet.

stories.title: Toronto shooting: two dead and four injured at Salsa on St Clair street festival
stories.summary: Police say two people exchanged gunfire in shooting that mayor called an ‘irresponsible act of violence’ in festival attended by families A shooting near a Toronto street festival killed two men and wounded four other people on Saturday evening, police said, adding that what initially prompted an active-shooter warning was an exchange of gunfire between two people targeting each other. Toronto police deputy chief Frank Barredo said investigators recovered two firearms after the shooting, which w

stories.title: A revolution in ruins: fury amid the rubble of a housing project in quake-hit Venezuela
stories.summary: Discontent with Trump-backed government mounts as Chávez heirs struggle to respond to disaster for which they seem ill-prepared Even before two powerful earthquakes reduced the OPPE 25 government housing project to an anarchy of shattered concrete and broken lives, the foundations of Hugo Chávez’s populist “Bolivarian” revolution were shaking in what was once a hotbed of support. Gabriel González remembers his elation when, in 2013, he received the keys to his freshly completed apartment in one

stories.title: Trump signals support for Patriot production in Ukraine
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump says he may let Ukraine produce Patriot air defense missiles. Analysts welcome the move as a sign of support for the country — but warn it could take years before production begins.

stories.title: US Senator Lindsey Graham dies after 'brief and sudden illness'
stories.summary: Graham, a prominent Republican voice on foreign policy, had recently returned from Ukraine, where he met with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy in Kyiv.

stories.title: Venezuela quake death toll passes 4,300 as scale of recovery effort looms large
stories.summary: Nearly 17,000 injured and thousands more listed as missing amid calls by president Delcy Rodríguez and UN for financial help The death toll in Venezuela’s devastating twin earthquakes has passed 4,300, the government said on Saturday. At least 4,333 people were killed and 16,740 injured in the back-to-back quakes on 24 June that flattened entire districts in the coastal state of La Guaira, the Venezuelan parliament chief, Jorge Rodríguez, wrote on Telegram. Thousands more people are listed as mi

stories.title: First patients enrolled in record-breaking Ebola treatment trial in DRC
stories.summary: Two drugs are being trialled in the Ituri region in a programme set up just six weeks after the outbreak was declared, with hopes it will reduce mortality rates There is no approved drug to help the medical teams scrabbling to save lives in the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo – but there are hopes that could change within months as the first patients are enrolled in a treatment trial. It is a record pace to set up and start this kind of research, scientists said, with pati

stories.title: France and UK to increase staffing at border controls in effort to avert travel chaos
stories.summary: Disruption at Channel crossings expected to rise amid new fingerprint and facial recognition checks France and the UK have agreed to increase staffing at border controls in response to warnings of travel chaos caused by new fingerprinting and facial recognition checks. Disruption at Channel crossings is expected to rise sharply next weekend at the start of the summer holiday season, with MPs saying there would be “utter chaos and miles of tailbacks” unless the EU’s entry-exit system (EES) is fix

stories.title: Heat is especially hard on people taking antidepressants
stories.summary: High temperatures can increase the risk of dizziness, dehydration, exhaustion and other heat-related problems for people taking antidepressants.

stories.title: Gas queues grow as Ukraine targets Russia's fuel supply
stories.summary: Attacks by Ukraine have triggered a fuel crisis in Russia. Reports from across Russia show how residents are dealing with the disruptions in their daily lives.

stories.title: How a Jewish heir is taking on Germany's far-right AfD
stories.summary: The AfD is using the iconic GDR-era Simson moped in its campaign. Now, a Jewish heir to the founding family is fighting to protect the company's legacy.

stories.title: Germany news: Neuschwanstein killer fights extradition to US
stories.summary: A man who murdered a woman at Neuschwanstein Castle says he fears execution in the US. Germany has been hit by another heat wave, though no records are likely to be broken this time, forecasters say.

stories.title: Germany's Ruhr Valley: Cities on brink of financial collapse
stories.summary: With tax revenues down and social spending up, more and more cities and towns in the Ruhrgebiet are struggling to make ends meet. One city that is particularly deep in the red is Oberhausen.

stories.title: California's largest lake is turning to dust
stories.summary: The Salton Sea is drying up, worsening air pollution for people living near its shores. Authorities are working to stabilize the lakebed and lock down the dust. But for some families, it's too late.

stories.title: How a viral seal called Neil caused mayhem in Australia
stories.summary: The five-year-old elephant seal attracted global attention during his recent time ashore in southern Tasmania.

stories.title: At least two killed in Toronto street festival shooting
stories.summary: Police say there was an exchange of gunfire between two individuals at the festival, and two firearms had been recovered from the scene.

stories.title: Iran attacks Gulf states to impose its Hormuz battle lines
stories.summary: Iran launched waves of attacks across the Gulf in response to US airstrikes the night before, signalling the Islamic Republic’s refreshed strike capabilities, while claiming sole control of the Strait of Hormuz. The Gulf states are once again on the frontlines of the growing US-Iran confrontation.

stories.title: Former Qatar ruler Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani dies aged 74
stories.summary: Sheikh Hamad was known for having helped bring about huge economic, social, and cultural change in Qatar during his reign and helping establish the nation as a global player in diplomacy and investment.

stories.title: People flee as Russian strike hits near Ukraine coffee shop
stories.summary: Three cameras caught the moment people ducked then ran for cover in Sumy, in north eastern Ukraine.

stories.title: Argentina fans erupt as Messi's men reach World Cup semifinal
stories.summary: Argentina fans celebrate in Buenos Aires after a dramatic 3-1 extra-time win over ten-man Switzerland sends the defending champions into the World Cup semifinals.

stories.title: Jude Bellingham double sends jubilant England into World Cup semis
stories.summary: England beat Norway 2-1 in extra time on 12 July 2026 to reach the World Cup semifinals. Fans celebrated in London after a Jude Bellingham double sent the Three Lions through to face Argentina.

stories.title: Nigeria says army has killed 300 bandits in north-western state of Zamfara
stories.summary: Vigilantes also took part in the fight that raged all night and the following morning, residents say Nigerian soldiers killed more than 300 members of kidnapping and cattle bandit gangs in the north-western state of Zamfara this week, according to a government official. Government troops targeted the gangs in Gummi district in a two-day operation that “led to the elimination of more than 300 terrorists”, Zamfara’s information commissioner, Mahmud Muhammad Dantawasa, said in a statement. Continue

stories.title: Germany: Left-wing group says it is behind railway sabotage
stories.summary: The "Angry Birds Commando" group said they placed incendiary devices on a railroad between the two busy cities of Cologne and Düsseldorf. The group blamed industrial technology for the destruction of the environment.

stories.title: Georgia left off NATO summit partner list as critics decry isolation
stories.summary: The Georgian government is facing accusations of international isolation after not being invited to the NATO summit in Turkey, the first time a key partner has been excluded from the alliance's agenda.

stories.title: War in Ukraine: Kyiv hit by Russian ballistic missiles and drone swarm, 10 injured
stories.summary: Ukraine woke to fresh destruction on 11 July after Russia launched overnight strikes on Kyiv, leaving at least 10 people injured, including a child, according to Ukrainian officials.

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: The US’s 250th anniversary, the funeral of Ali Khamenei, another brutal heatwave in Europe and the World Cup – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Continue reading...

stories.title: UK police arrest murder suspect in Ann Widdecombe's death
stories.summary: Police in southwest England say they have arrested a 26-year-old man on suspicion of murdering politician Ann Widdecombe. Based on initial inquiries, they were not treating the case as terrorism or politically motivated.

stories.title: Germany's richest man takes on Big Tech
stories.summary: Dieter Schwarz made his fortune from supermarkets, becoming Germany's wealthiest person. Now he wants to take on Google, Microsoft and Amazon and an entire region stands to benefit.

stories.title: China achieves first-ever controlled rocket booster recovery
stories.summary: China successfully recaptured the first stage of a rocket after a launch on Friday in a breakthrough for the country's space program, state media said. The first stage of a Long March-10B rocket separated from the second stage after liftoff and returned to a platform in the sea.

stories.title: Crews drain Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool again as part of troubled Trump revamp
stories.summary: Trump directed that the bottom be painted what he called “American flag blue” but problems began quickly after the initial work was finished.

stories.title: Japanese school teaches children how to stay safe around wild bears
stories.summary: Children at a primary school in central Japan are taking part in a bear awareness programme, learning how to safely coexist with wild bears as sightings remain common in the area.

stories.title: Spain fans celebrate World Cup semi-final place after Belgium win
stories.summary: Spain celebrated a dramatic 2-1 victory over Belgium on 10 July as fans filled Madrid to cheer La Roja into the FIFA World Cup semi-finals.

stories.title: Trump threatens to 'completely decimate' Iran, a day after claiming talks to take place
stories.summary: "1,000 missiles are locked and loaded and aimed at the Islamic Republic of Iran," Trump wrote on social media.

stories.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged theft of trade secrets - here's what to know
stories.summary: A spokesperson for OpenAI said in a statement: "We have no interest in other companies' trade secrets."

stories.title: 'We escaped Spanish wildfire, but our friends lost their lives'
stories.summary: Hundreds of firefighters in Spain have been battling a deadly wildfire in the south of the country.

stories.title: A rock star welcome and human rights protests to greet India’s prime minister Narendra Modi in Australia
stories.summary: Amnesty International says Albanese has opportunity to ‘reaffirm mutual commitment’ to rule of law as thousands in diaspora expected at stadium event Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Indian prime minister Narendra Modi’s first visit to Australia in three years has sparked calls for the federal government to raise human rights concerns, as excitement builds within the nation’s Indian diaspora. Modi’s visit begi

stories.title: Attacks on Kyiv ahead of NATO summit: What is Putin's goal?
stories.summary: Kyiv suffered heavy Russian strikes ahead of the NATO summit in Ankara. Experts say Russian President Vladimir Putin is trying to send a message.

stories.title: Merz: Germany agreed to buy Tomahawk missiles at NATO summit
stories.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz has informed the Bundestag parliament of a deal to buy US Tomahawk cruise missiles and station them in Germany. He said this closed a "important strategic ‌gap ​in our defense."

stories.title: EU rejects suspending biometric border controls despite 20 ‘difficult spots’
stories.summary: Officials admit new post-Brexit EES system is ‘not perfect’, as airports and airlines voice fears over delays The EU has rejected calls by airports and airlines to suspend the implementation of new fingerprinting and facial recognition border controls even though it admits there are “20 difficult spots” with queue chaos. With only a week to go before the peak summer holiday season starts, EU officials said the new entry/exit system (EES) was “not perfect” but it would tell travel industry repres

stories.title: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen says she will run for president in 2027 – video
stories.summary: Speaking in a prime-time interview on TF1 TV on Tuesday, the French far-right leader Marine Le Pen announced she will run for president in 2027 after an appeal court shortened her ban on holding public office. On Tuesday, the Paris appeals court upheld Le Pen's conviction for misusing European parliament funds but reduced the ban on running for office, clearing the way for the 57-year-old to stand in next year's election Continue reading...

stories.title: Furore in Nigeria over fake federal agency set up in government HQ
stories.summary: President orders investigation after fictitious body given funding, triggering renewed scrutiny of alleged corruption A fictitious federal entity that was allocated 1.3bn naira (£700,000) in Nigeria’s 2026 budget has precipitated a political storm in Africa’s largest democracy in the run-up to a general election set for January. The fake agency came to light last October when Femi Gbajabiamila, the president’s chief of staff, wrote to the police alleging that his signature, along with official s

stories.title: Mexico investigates whether US lied about role in capture of drug lord
stories.summary: Reporting suggests FBI involved in seizure of Ismael ‘El Mayo’ Zambada García from Mexican territory in 2024 Mexico has launched an investigation into whether the US lied about its involvement in the capture and secretive transfer of a top Sinaloa cartel member in 2024, in what would be a potential violation of the country’s sovereignty. The US has long denied it played any role in the operation to detain the drug lord Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada García, a founder of the Sinaloa cartel, inside Mexi

stories.title: Protests engulf Indian state after rape and murder of 11-year-old girl
stories.summary: Innocent man lynched by mob in West Bengal as police killing of suspect further escalates tensions Protests have engulfed the Indian state of West Bengal after the rape and murder of an 11-year-old girl, the subsequent lynching of an innocent man and the police killing of one of the accused. Outrage erupted on Sunday after the body of a missing girl was recovered from a pond in a town just outside the state capital, Kolkata. Continue reading...

stories.title: Violence on trains in Germany is on the rise
stories.summary: The verdict in the trial over the killing of a train conductor highlights growing aggression toward railway employees in Germany. What is causing it — and what can be done about it?

stories.title: Mosque replica and anti-immigration signs appear on Northern Ireland bonfire
stories.summary: A loyalist bonfire in Moygashel, Northern Ireland displays a mosque replica and anti-immigration signs ahead of Orange Order celebrations.

stories.title: South Florida's Palm Beach airport renamed President Donald J. Trump International
stories.summary: Since returning to the White House, Trump has taken repeated measures to put his name and image in official spaces, an abrupt break with American political tradition.

stories.title: Dowry murders in India no longer spark public anger or debate, study finds
stories.summary: Thousands of women are killed in dowry disputes each year, despite the practice being banned in 1961 Dowry deaths in India no longer provoke the public anger they once did, despite thousands of women’s lives still being lost every year, according to new research. The killings – women who are murdered or driven to suicide following dowry disputes between families – have also faded from political debate, despite an increase in cases. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK judges begin hearing appeal over Trinidad and Tobago anti-gay law
stories.summary: Activist is challenging ruling last year that restored colonial-era homophobic law against same-sex intimacy Some of the UK’s top judges are hearing arguments over whether a Trinidad and Tobago court had the legal right to overturn a 2018 ruling to remove colonial-era homophobic laws that criminalise anal sex between consenting men. The country’s “ buggery law ”, often referred to as its “sodomy” law, was created in 1925 and was written into Trinidad and Tobago’s 1986 Sexual Offences Act . In 20

stories.title: Pakistan locates wreckage of Boeing 737 cargo plane that went missing off coast
stories.summary: Early flight data shows K2 Airways plane crashed into sea with five crew on board south-west of Karachi Pakistan has located the wreckage of a Boeing cargo plane, the country’s airports authority said, adding that rescuers were searching for the five crew members on board when the aircraft went missing. The plane was approaching Karachi from Sharjah in the United Arab Emirates when radar showed it “rapidly descending” on Tuesday evening after reporting a “navigational system issue”, according to

stories.title: US charges Indian criminal gang leader with organising murder of Canadian Sikh activist
stories.summary: Lawrence Bishnoi, who is in prison in India, is accused of orchestrating assassination of Hardeep Singh Nijjar in 2023 US and Canadian authorities say they have “dismantled” the leadership of a notorious Indian criminal group, charging dozens of operatives who have “inflicted pain and cruelty on people, victims around the globe”, including a high-profile murder in Canada that strained diplomatic relations between Canada and India. At a press conference on Tuesday, members of the FBI and Canada’s

stories.title: ‘A lot of red flags’: plans for New Zealand’s first AI datacentre spark concern as locals demand greater transparency
stories.summary: Plans to build a NZ$3.5bn datacentre in Makarewa in the country’s south has drawn concern about electricity and water use, and potential noise pollution People living near the site of New Zealand’s first planned AI datacentre are calling for more transparency about the project, especially about how the centre’s huge electricity and water use and potential noise pollution could affect them. Singapore-based company Datagrid has secured approval to build a NZ$3.5bn (US$2bn) AI datacentre on a 49-he

stories.title: Heatwave across western Europe – in pictures
stories.summary: Western Europe has been scorched by its hottest June on record, scientists have said, as the UK enters its third heatwave of the year and wildfires ravage France and Spain Western Europe records hottest-ever June as heatwaves intensify Continue reading...

stories.title: South Korea chip maker SK hynix rides AI boom raising $26.5bn in huge US listing
stories.summary: SK hynix, a supplier of advanced memory chips, has seen profits skyrocket thanks to the global race to build AI datacentres South Korean chip maker SK hynix set pricing for its mega US listing on Friday, aiming to raise $26.5bn as it takes advantage of the AI boom in what will be one of the world’s biggest ever stock sales. The Asian semiconductor giant plans to issue the equivalent of about 18m shares on Wall Street’s tech-heavy Nasdaq index later in the day. Continue reading...

stories.title: Developing countries spend more repaying foreign debt than on education, UN reveals
stories.summary: Unesco report shows children lost out to servicing debt in 113 countries, with 18 spending five times more on loans Most developing countries spent less on education than they did repaying debt last year, according to the UN, at the same time as global aid to education is predicted to decline by up to 30%. More was spent on servicing foreign debt than on education in 113 developing countries in 2025, according to research by the UN’s culture and education agency, Unesco. In sub-Saharan Africa, c

stories.title: Weather tracker: Typhoon leaves people stranded on rooftops in China
stories.summary: Eleven reported dead as flooding also brings danger of snakes, while buildings collapse in Mumbai amid heavy rain As the first typhoon to make landfall in China for the 2026 season, Maysak has caused devastating damage in southern and central regions. The Guangxi region received intense downpours of up to 280mm in 12 hours, causing rivers to swell and dam walls to break. By Monday morning, flooding across the city of Nanning and surrounding areas had resulted in many people being stranded on roo

stories.title: Conflict, climate change, cash-strapped: Why poverty persists
stories.summary: With just under five years left to complete the UN's Sustainable Development Goals, a new report shows what's hampering progress. And what the solutions are.

stories.title: Brain tumors: New vaccine offers patients hope for more time
stories.summary: A new therapy offers people with seemingly incurable brain tumors scope for hope: It could slow the progression of the disease and extend patients' lives.
